steps.title: Use the delayed-ring result
steps.content: In the simulation, delaying the middle ring caused oversized super-Earths to develop from the innermost ring. Because Earth belongs to the group formed from that innermost ring, the result connects the middle ring's timing to the eventual size of Earth-like inner planets.

steps.title: Rule out unsupported hypotheses
steps.content: Choice A makes an unsupported comparison with initial simulations; Choice C switches to the outermost ring; Choice D contradicts the text's division of planet-forming materials. The hypothesis left by the experiment is that middle-ring timing affected Earth's size. The answer is B.
